name: Leo Wright
description: American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist and flautist (1933-1991)
abstract: Leo Wright (December 14, 1933 in Wichita Falls, Texas – January 4, 1991 in Vienna) was an American jazz musician who played alto saxophone, flute and clarinet. He played with Booker Ervin, Charles Mingus, John Hardee, Kenny Burrell, Johnny Coles, Blue Mitchell and Dizzy Gillespie in the late 1950s, early 1960s and in the late 1970s.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dizzy Gillespie: Genius at Midnight

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: when I was in Berlin I worked with Leo Wright. Leo Wright and Carmell Jones. We were in one of the radio bands together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: Along with myself it was Eddie Harris and Leo Wright and Don Ellis and Cedar Walton. So it was really playing with good musicians
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: That’s Leo Wright, Carmell Jones, Åke Persson, all these guys... I want you to play in the radio band in Berlin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: It was Leo Wright... But he came back after that, and that’s when he joined Dizzy... One album we did called “Have Trumpet Will Excite” features that a lot, the two of them
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Watts
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. Did Leo Wright play with you, Melvin's son? Was he too young then? Who? He's a kid. Oh, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: I'm not sure he was ever with us in the, in the jazz band. But uh, yeah, uh, Leo was a great musician
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
